title: System and method for object and location-related delivery of real-time messages
abstract: A system and method are described. In an example, the system includes a communication device that is associated with a user, one or more tags that are associated with objects and/or locations distributed throughout a physical space, and a messaging management server that is configured to generate and supply messages to the communication device of a user based on activity detected about the user with respect to the physical space.
claims:
Claims What Is Claimed Is: 1. A system, comprising: a plurality of tags, each tag from the plurality of tags being associated with an object or location; a network interface configured to receive information from the plurality of tags and further configured to exchange messages with communication devices that are associated with users; a processor coupled with the network interface; and computer memory coupled with the processor, wherein the computer memory includes processor-executable instructions that comprise: a system management instruction set configured to determine a status of the object or location based on the information received from the plurality of tags; and a message delivery instruction set configured to determine that a user is positioned within proximity of a particular object or location, identify a message to deliver to the user based on a unique user/object or location pair established between the user and the particular object or location, determine a communication capability of a communication device associated with the user, and based on the unique user/object or location pair and further based on the communication capability of the communication device, deliver the identified message to the communication device while the user is positioned within proximity of the particular object or location. 2. The system of claim 1, wherein the message delivery instruction set is further configured to determine a content of the message based on a condition of the particular object or location. 3. The system of claim 2, wherein the condition of the particular object or location is determined based on sensor information received from a sensor that is associated with the particular object or location and that converts a physical property around the object or location into an electronic signal. 4. The system of claim 2, wherein the condition of the particular object or location is determined based on a note received from a different user that was previously within proximity of the particular object or location and wherein the note is stored in a message database and further referenced by the message delivery instruction set in 

 response to determining that the user is positioned within proximity of the particular object or location. 5. The system of claim 1, wherein the communication device comprises a wearable communication device that is uniquely associated with the user and wherein the identified message is delivered to the wearable communication device via a wireless communication protocol. 6. The system of claim 1, wherein the instructions further comprise: a user behavior instruction set configured to monitor user behavior activity of the users and determine a normal behavior model for at least one user relative to the plurality of tags. 7. The system of claim 6, wherein the user behavior instruction set is further configured to determine, on a per-user basis, normal behavior models for the users relative to the plurality of tags and wherein the message delivery instruction set is configured to identify the message to deliver to the user based on a reference to a normal behavior model for the user. 8. The system of claim 6, wherein the normal behavior model is updated in response to determining that a new normal behavior exists for the at least one user relative to the plurality of tags. 9. The system of claim 1, wherein the information received from the plurality of tags at the network interface is received via a communication network and via the communication devices that are associated with the users. 10. The system of claim 9, wherein the plurality of tags are configured to pair with the communication devices and exchange information with the communication devices via a proximity -based communication protocol. 11. The system of claim 10, wherein the information exchanged between the plurality of tags and the communication devices comprises a tag URL stored in memory of the tag and wherein the tag URL comprises a reference to an identifier of the associated object or location. 12. The system of claim 10, wherein the information exchanged between the plurality of tags and the communication devices comprises sensor information received from a sensor that is associated with one of the plurality of tags. 13. A method, comprising: receiving, at a network interface, information from a plurality of tags; exchanging, via the network interface, messages with communication devices that are associated with users; determining, with a processor, a status of a particular object or location based on the information received from the plurality of tags; determining, with a processor, that a user is positioned within proximity of the particular object or location based on the messages exchanged with the communication devices; identifying, with a processor, a message to deliver to the user based on a unique user/object or location pair established between the user and the particular object or location; determining, with a processor, a communication capability of a communication device associated with the user; and based on the unique user/object or location pair and further based on the communication capability of the communication device, delivering, via the network interface, the identified message to the communication device while the user is positioned within proximity of the particular object or location. 14. The method of claim 13, further comprising: determining, with the processor, a content of the message based on a condition of the particular object or location, wherein the condition of the particular object or location is determined based on sensor information received from a sensor that is associated with the particular object or location and that converts a physical property around the object or location into an electronic signal. 15. The method of claim 14, wherein the condition of the particular object or location is further determined based on a note received from a different user that was previously within proximity of the particular object or location and wherein the note is stored in a message database and further referenced by the message delivery instruction set in response to determining that the user is positioned within proximity of the particular object or location. 16. The method of claim 14, further comprising: monitoring, with the processor, user behavior activity of the users; and 

 determining, with the processor, a normal behavior model for at least one user relative to the plurality of tags. 17. The method of claim 16, further comprising: determining, on a per-user basis and with the processor, normal behavior models for the users relative to the plurality of tags and wherein the message delivery instruction set is configured to identify the message to deliver to the user based on a reference to a normal behavior model for the user; and updating, with the processor, the normal behavior model in response to determining that a new normal behavior exists for the at least one user relative to the plurality of tags. 18. The method of claim 13, wherein the information received from the plurality of tags at the network interface is received via a communication network and via the communication devices that are associated with the users. 19. The method of claim 18, wherein the plurality of tags are configured to pair with the communication devices and exchange information with the communication devices via a proximity -based communication protocol. 20. The method of claim 19, wherein the information exchanged between the plurality of tags and the communication devices comprises a tag URL stored in memory of the tag and wherein the tag URL comprises a reference to an identifier of the associated object or location.
